LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JENNIFER GILES' MOTION TO DISMISS APPEAL OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S ORDER, DECISION OR AWARD. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED AS MOOT. DEFENDANTS DISMISSED THE APPEAL ON MARCH 10, 2014. THE COURT WILL RULE ON THE ATTORNEY'S FEES ISSUE IF AND WHEN A MOTION FOR FEES IS FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976
DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF GILES, JENNIFER NICOLE

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER GILES' MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. THE LABOR COMMISSIONER SERVED THE ORDER, DECISION, OR AWARD ("ODA") ON JANUARY 24, 2014. DEFENDANTS FILED THEIR NOTICE OF APPEAL ON FEBRUARY 7, 2014, THE LAST DAY THAT AN APPEAL OF THE ODA COULD BE FILED PER LAB. CODE SEC. 98.2(A). DEFENDANTS FAILED TO POST AN UNDERTAKING, AS REQUIRED BY LAB. CODE SEC. 98.2(B). THE POSTING OF AN UNDERTAKING IS MANDATORY IN ORDER FOR THE SUPERIOR COURT TO OBTAIN JURISDICTION FOR THE APPEAL AND THE COURT LACKS AUTHORITY TO EXTEND THE DEADLINE TO POST THE UNDERTAKING. (PALAGIN V. PANIGUA CONSTRUCTION, INC. (2013) 222 CAL.APP.4TH 124, 140.) THEREFORE, THERE WAS NO WAY THAT THE APPEAL COULD HAVE GONE FORWARD. NONETHELESS, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BEGAN PREPARING FOR THE APPEAL AND NOW SEEKS TO RECOVER FEES FOR THAT PREPARATION. IT WOULD BE INEQUITABLE TO REQUIRE DEFENDANTS TO BEAR THE BURDEN OF THESE COSTS. ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES PERTAINING TO THE MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF COULD HAVE SIMPLY FILED AN EX PARTE APPLICATION TO HAVE THE NOTICE OF APPEAL STRICKEN, BUT FILED A NOTICED MOTION IN ORDER TO ASSERT HER ARGUMENTS FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. AGAIN, IT WOULD BE INEQUITABLE TO REQUIRE DEFENDANTS TO BEAR THE BURDEN OF THESE COSTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
M. Colleen Ryan, Esq. of the California Department of Industrial Relations, Tel. 415.703.4868, for plaintiff and moving party;
Paul R. Lynd, Esq. of Nixon Peabody LLP, Tel. 415.984.8235, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER GILES' Motion For Reasonable Attorney's Fees is Denied. The Labor Commissioner served the Order, Decision, or Award ("ODA") on January 24, 2014. Defendants filed their Notice of Appeal on February 7, 2014, the last day that an appeal of the ODA could be filed per Lab. Code sec. 98.2(a). Defendants failed to post an undertaking, as required by Lab. Code sec. 98.2(b). The posting of an undertaking is mandatory in order for the superior court to obtain jurisdiction for the appeal and the court lacks authority to extend the deadline to post the undertaking. (Palagin v. Panigua Construction, Inc. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 124, 140.) Therefore, there was no way that the appeal could have gone forward. Nonetheless, Plaintiff's counsel began preparing for the appeal and now seeks to recover fees for that preparation. It would be inequitable to require Defendants to bear the burden of these costs. Additionally, Plaintiff's request for fees pertaining to the motion to dismiss is denied. Plaintiff could have simply filed an ex parte application to have the Notice of Appeal stricken, but filed a noticed motion in order to assert her arguments for attorney's fees. Again, it would be inequitable to require Defendants to bear the burden of these costs. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2014 Vacated

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Appeal Of The Labor Commissioner'S Order, Decision Or Award
Off Calendar Mar-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: M. Colleen Ryan, Esq., 415.703.4868, for Appellant Jennifer Giles.
Paul Lynd, Esq., 415.984.8235, for Respondents 3R Company, LP and Mary Ravetti.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER GILES' Motion To Dismiss Appeal Of The Labor Commissioner'S Order, Decision Or Award is denied as moot. Defendants dismissed the appeal on March 10, 2014. The Court will rule on the attorney's fees issue if and when a motion for fees is filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
